opp.name: Fragua_S2P_May'26
opp.next_step: 6/22 MC – Confirmed meeting with Miroslava, Christian, Olivia, and Jorge for Tuesday at 11:00 AM MT. Objective is to conduct an initial business discovery session to better understand the initiative, key drivers, stakeholders, current challenges, and overall business objectives. Aligned with the client that this discussion will remain at a high level from a business perspective, while a deeper process and technical discussion will take place once the NDA is fully executed. NDA review remains in progress, and we are expecting the signed agreement back as early as tomorrow. Primary goal for this session is to establish business alignment, and position Zycus for the next phase of discovery.
opp.ais_why:
• No Avoma recordings exist for Fragua; the two scheduled meetings either had no conferencing details or were cancelled, so there is no transcript evidence of customer AI interest.
• SF next step describes a procurement transformation RFP covering sourcing, supplier risk, eProcurement, and spend analysis - broad scope but no AI, agentic, or Merlin cues in the narrative.
• Opp is brand-new (created 5/7) at Qualified with onsite discovery still to be scheduled, so absence of AI signal is a data-quality artifact more than a clear stance.

sources: Salesforce Next Step 22 Jun 2026
Avoma call 15 Jun 2026 (internal sync, no buyer pain captured)
Salesforce fields Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c (null)
metrics.narrative: No quantified business impact or ROI model documented. The 22 Jun Next Step describes the 23 Jun discovery session objective as understanding 'the initiative, key drivers, stakeholders, current challenges, and overall business objectives', which signals the pain-discovery phase is only just beginning. No Salesforce field (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c) carries quantified cost drivers, headcount savings, cycle-time reduction, or error-rate impact. The one available call transcript (15 Jun internal sync with Mario) discusses Zycus internal staffing and AI-feature policy, not buyer pain. Without a financial anchor—current-state cost drivers, manual-process burden, supplier-management inefficiencies, or savings targets—the buyer has no basis to justify $200K investment or negotiate commercial terms. This vulnerability will surface when pricing discussions begin. The highest-leverage immediate move is to conduct a structured pain-quantification session in the next discovery touchpoint, capturing current-state costs (PO cycle time, maverick spend, supplier onboarding delays, compliance penalties) and building a joint ROI model with the buyer's finance/procurement leads.

sources: Salesforce Next Step 22 Jun 2026 (Miroslava organizing discovery session)
Recent task contacts (Miroslava Sarahi)
Account-level contacts: Julio García (Director), Claudia Hermosillo (Gerente)—not yet engaged
Avoma manifest (no recorded call showing Miroslava advocacy behaviors)
champion.narrative: Miroslava Sarahi (Analyst of Procurement Negotiations) is the primary contact and is developing as a potential champion. She organized the 23 Jun discovery session and has been the consistent thread across recent activity (22 Jun Next Step names her; she appears in recent task contacts per the buyer-identity block). However, her role is analyst-level, which limits her authority to drive budget approval, executive sponsorship, or internal advocacy against competitors. There is no evidence yet of champion-level advocacy behaviors: no introductions to the Economic Buyer or Decision Maker, no shared internal documents (e.g., the buyer's business case or evaluation scorecard), no active pushing back on competitors on Zycus's behalf, and no evidence she is driving the agenda internally. The 23 Jun discovery session included Christian, Olivia, and Jorge (roles unknown), which signals Miroslava is facilitating multi-stakeholder access—a positive sign—but her influence over those stakeholders is unconfirmed. The account-level contacts include Julio García (Director de Cadena de Suministro) and Claudia Hermosillo (Gerente Compras Institucionales), both of whom are senior enough to champion the deal and influence budget approval, but neither is yet engaged. Champion strength is 'developing' because Miroslava is engaged and facilitating access, but she lacks the seniority and demonstrated advocacy to be considered a strong champion. The immediate move is to ask Miroslava in the next touchpoint: 'Who internally is the strongest advocate for this initiative, and who will help us navigate the approval process?' This surfaces the real champion or signals the need to build one at a higher level (e.g., Julio García).

sources: Salesforce fields Competitors__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c (null)
Avoma call 15 Jun 2026 (internal sync, no competitor mention)
Avoma manifest touchpoints 5 May, 17 Jun, 23 Jun (no recorded transcripts capturing competitive references)
competition.narrative: No competitive intelligence documented 50 days into the deal. No Salesforce field (Competitors__c, Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c, How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c) names a competitor or incumbent. The one available call transcript (15 Jun internal Zycus sync) does not mention competitors. The Next Step and other Avoma touchpoints (5 May, 17 Jun, 23 Jun) have no recorded transcripts that could capture competitive references. The buyer's current state (are they replacing an incumbent P2P/S2P system, consolidating multiple tools, or implementing for the first time?) is unknown. Without competitive intelligence, Zycus cannot position differentiation (e.g., Merlin AI negotiation agent vs SAP Ariba's static workflows, or iCompliance vs Coupa's limited risk module) or anticipate competitor objections. This vulnerability will surface if a competitor (e.g., SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, Jaggaer) frames the narrative first or if the buyer is running a multi-vendor RFP and Zycus is unaware. The immediate move is to ask the buying committee in the next touchpoint: 'Are you evaluating other vendors alongside Zycus, and are you replacing an existing system or implementing S2P for the first time?' This surfaces the competitive landscape and the buyer's current-state baseline, which are essential inputs for positioning and pricing strategy.

sources: Salesforce Next Step 22 Jun 2026 (pain discovery objective, outcome not yet captured)
Avoma call 15 Jun 2026 (internal sync, no buyer pain)
Salesforce fields Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Gaps_identified_during_sales_demo__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c (null)
identify_pain.narrative: Specific operational or financial pain is not yet documented. The 22 Jun Next Step states the 23 Jun discovery session objective as 'understand the initiative, key drivers, stakeholders, current challenges, and overall business objectives', which signals pain discovery is the session's purpose but the outcome is not yet captured in Salesforce or a recorded call. No Salesforce field (Pain_points_in_the_current_solution__c, Gaps_identified_during_sales_demo__c, What_if_this_is_not_done__c) carries the buyer's articulated pain. The only available call transcript (15 Jun internal Zycus sync with Mario) discusses Zycus internal matters (vacation policies, AI-feature disable request for Ana Karen), not buyer pain. The product scope (full S2P suite: iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eProcurement, iRequest, ML iAnalyze, Merlin, iRisk, iCompliance) implies the buyer is likely experiencing pain across sourcing inefficiency, manual procurement workflows, supplier-management fragmentation, spend-visibility gaps, compliance risk, and negotiation inefficiencies—but none of this is confirmed by the buyer on a call or in a field. The pain owner is also unknown (is it procurement ops, supply-chain leadership, finance, or IT?). The immediate move is to debrief the 23 Jun discovery session with the deal owner (Karson Keogh) and extract the buyer's stated pain points, then document them in Salesforce and build a pain-to-solution map for the next touchpoint.

sources: Salesforce Next Step 22 Jun 2026 (NDA in progress, signature expected imminently)
Account-level contacts: Eduardo Rosas (Controller), Adonay Mosqueda Flores (IT Vendor Manager)—not yet engaged
Salesforce fields Current_Contract_Expiration__c, Mandate__c (null)
paper_process.narrative: Paper process is partially visible: NDA execution is in progress as of 22 Jun, with signature expected imminently ('we are expecting the signed agreement back as early as tomorrow', per 22 Jun Next Step). The NDA is gating deeper technical and process discussions, which signals the buyer treats this as a formal procurement with legal and InfoSec review stages ahead. However, the downstream contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, Order Form, legal redline process, InfoSec questionnaire, ERP/systems integration scoping) are not yet mapped. No Salesforce field (Current_Contract_Expiration__c, Mandate__c) indicates whether this is a replacement of an incumbent system (which would provide a forcing-function timeline) or a greenfield implementation. The account-level contacts include Eduardo Rosas (Controller) and Adonay Mosqueda Flores (IT Vendor Manager), both of whom would typically be involved in contract review and systems integration; neither is yet engaged in the opportunity. The immediate move is to ask the buying committee in the next touchpoint: 'Once we complete discovery and present a proposal, what is your contract-review process—legal, InfoSec, IT integration approval—and what are the typical review cycles?' This creates visibility into the back-half timeline and surfaces any procurement-channel or partner-involvement requirements.

sources: Salesforce Next Step 22 Jun 2026 (Miroslava, Christian, Olivia, Jorge—no EB-level role)
Account-level contacts: Julio García (Director), Claudia Hermosillo (Gerente)—not yet engaged in opp
Salesforce fields X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c (null)
economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer identified or engaged. The 23 Jun discovery session included Miroslava (Analyst of Procurement Negotiations), Christian, Olivia, and Jorge, per the 22 Jun Next Step; none of these roles carry budget authority for a $200K enterprise SaaS investment. Miroslava is analyst-level; Christian, Olivia, and Jorge are not titled in any Salesforce contact role or account-level contact record, so their seniority and authority are unknown. The account-level contacts include Julio García (Director de Cadena de Suministro) and Claudia Hermosillo (Gerente Compras Institucionales), both of whom could plausibly hold or influence budget approval, but neither has been engaged in a recorded buyer-facing call or added to the opportunity contact roles. No Salesforce field (X10a_Sponsor__c, Who_will_approve_budget__c, Executive_Sponsor_Identified__c) names the EB. For a $200K enterprise S2P deal in a large LATAM account (Corporativo Fragua / Cargill affiliation), budget approval will require Director or VP-level sponsorship and likely CFO sign-off. Without EB identification and active engagement in the next 14 days, pricing and commercial discussions will stall. The immediate move is to ask Miroslava directly in the post-discovery follow-up: 'Who will approve the budget and sign the contract for this initiative?' and to request an introduction to that person within 7 days.

sources: Salesforce Next Step 22 Jun 2026 (NDA-gated deeper discovery)
Avoma manifest touchpoints 5 May, 17 Jun, 23 Jun (multi-stakeholder committee forming)
Account structure: Corporativo Fragua SAB de CV / Cargill email domains (complex approval likely)
Salesforce fields What_is_the_decision_process__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c (null)
decision_process.narrative: Decision process is forming but not yet mapped. The call sequence shows: 5 May (two touchpoints, cancelled/completed—likely initial contact or demo scheduling), 15 Jun (internal Zycus sync), 17 Jun (buyer touchpoint, no transcript), 23 Jun (formal discovery session with Miroslava, Christian, Olivia, Jorge). The 23 Jun session marks the first structured discovery touchpoint with multiple buyer stakeholders, signaling a multi-person buying committee is assembling. However, the process steps (demo → RFI/RFP → shortlist → pricing → approval chain) and the approval authority chain are not yet documented. The 22 Jun Next Step notes 'deeper process and technical discussion will take place once the NDA is fully executed', which implies the buyer is gating detailed evaluation behind the NDA—a formal procurement practice consistent with enterprise buying. No Salesforce field (What_is_the_decision_process__c, Purchase_approvals_Required_from__c, Multiple_approvals__c, Does_the_Buyer_need_approval__c) captures the process. The account affiliation (Corporativo Fragua / Cargill) suggests a complex approval chain may involve both Fragua local leadership and Cargill parent-company procurement/finance review. The immediate move is to ask the buying committee in the next touchpoint: 'Walk us through your evaluation and approval process—who reviews, who scores, who approves budget, and what are the key gates and timelines?' This creates a mutual close plan anchor.

sources: Salesforce Next Step 22 Jun 2026
Products field (full S2P suite scope)
Salesforce fields Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c (null)
decision_criteria.narrative: Decision criteria are in early discovery; the 22 Jun Next Step states the 23 Jun session objective as 'high-level business perspective' and 'better understand the initiative, key drivers, stakeholders, current challenges, and overall business objectives', signaling the criteria-mapping phase has just begun. No formal RFP, RFI, or structured evaluation scorecard is documented. The product scope (iSource, iSave, iSupplier, eProcurement, iRequest, ML iAnalyze, Merlin, iRisk, iCompliance, AppXtend) suggests the buyer is evaluating a full S2P suite, which implies criteria will span sourcing automation, supplier management, spend analytics, AI-driven negotiation (Merlin), risk monitoring, and compliance. However, the relative priority (e.g., is Merlin AI a must-have or a nice-to-have?), the buyer's current-state gaps, and the formal evaluation framework are not yet captured. No Salesforce field (Business_Requirements__c, Top_Challenges_Priorities__c, AI_Needs_in_RFP_Rating__c) carries the buyer's stated criteria. The next move is to run a structured use-case workshop in the coming 14 days, mapping the buyer's top 3-5 operational pain points to Zycus modules and establishing a weighted scorecard (e.g., 'reduce PO cycle time by 40%' maps to eProcurement + Merlin; 'supplier risk monitoring' maps to iRisk). This converts discovery from high-level to decision-grade.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI fit signal is unknown. No AIS fields populated (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c null). No recorded buyer-facing call captures the buyer's AI appetite, Merlin interest, or AI-driven use-case validation. Product scope includes Merlin and Merlin Intake, signaling Zycus has proposed AI capabilities, but buyer response is not documented. The 15 Jun internal call mentions an AI-feature policy constraint (disable request for Ana Karen), which is a Zycus internal matter, not a buyer signal. AI fit will be measurable after a Merlin demo or workshop, where the buyer's reaction to AI-driven negotiation, requisition intake, and spend analytics can be captured. Until then, AI Hungry/Curious/Resistant tier is unassigned.

confidence_signals.summary: Engagement is live and buyer-responsive. Discovery cadence is regular (touchpoints 5 May, 15 Jun, 17 Jun, 23 Jun, roughly every 7-10 days). Rep-initiated outreach 22 Jun confirmed formal discovery session for 23 Jun (completed per manifest touchpoint 5) with multi-stakeholder buying committee (Miroslava, Christian, Olivia, Jorge). Last Salesforce activity 25 Jun (1 day ago). NDA execution in progress, signature expected imminently. No cooling signals. However, 4 of 5 buyer touchpoints have no recorded transcript, limiting visibility into buyer pain, competitive landscape, and decision criteria. Deal is early-stage (50 days old, no EB/DM mapped, no quantified ROI), so confidence in close-date defensibility is low, but engagement momentum is positive.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 7 May 2026, 50 days old; in Formal Evaluation stage, close 30 Sep 2026 (96 days out). LastActivityDate 25 Jun (1 day ago), rep-initiated outreach 22 Jun confirms live engagement. Discovery session scheduled 23 Jun (3 days ago, completed per manifest touchpoint 5). Time-in-stage: 50 days in Formal Evaluation is consistent with early discovery in this enterprise S2P motion. No prior stage-date series available (qualified_date null). No forward slip recorded in field history. Current pace: discovery touchpoints 5 May, 15 Jun, 17 Jun, 23 Jun show regular cadence (every 7-10 days), but full cycle from discovery to close typically requires 12-15 months in large enterprise S2P deals. With 96 days remaining and no EB engaged, no pricing discussion initiated, no ROI model, and NDA still pending, the close date is optimistic. A healthy, engaged deal with insufficient runway.
evidence: Rep-initiated outreach 22 Jun (Next Step) confirmed discovery session for 23 Jun with Miroslava, Christian, Olivia, Jorge; objective was business discovery and stakeholder/challenge mapping. Awaiting post-session debrief and buyer follow-up actions.

Discovery touchpoints 5 May (2 cancelled/completed), 15 Jun (internal sync with Mario), 17 Jun (completed), 23 Jun (scheduled/completed) show live engagement cadence but no recorded transcripts for the buyer-facing sessions after 5 May.
NDA execution in progress as of 22 Jun; signature expected imminently per Next Step. Deeper technical discussions blocked until NDA signed.

No Economic Buyer, Decision Maker, or quantified value case documented. For a $200K enterprise S2P deal in LATAM requiring executive approval, 96 days is insufficient without EB sponsorship, pricing anchor, and ROI model in place.

Product scope confirmed: full S2P suite (iSource, iSupplier, eProcurement, iRequest, ML iAnalyze, iSave, Merlin, iRisk, Merlin Intake, iCompliance, AppXtend, iSaaS). Broad scope implies multi-stakeholder buying committee and extended contracting cycle.
north_star_verdict.headline: Discovery cadence every 7-10 days through June confirms live buyer interest, but 50 days in Formal Evaluation with NDA still pending, no EB mapped, no pricing or ROI anchored, and 96-day runway to September close signals insufficient progression velocity for.

flags: Map the Economic Buyer before commercial discussions. For a $200K enterprise S2P deal, budget approval will require Director or VP sign-off. Julio García (Director de Cadena de Suministro, account-level contact) is a candidate; use the post-NDA technical sessions to confirm who controls budget and pricing authority, and position an executive-to-executive conversation with the deal owner's manager if the EB is VP-level.

Discover the competitive landscape and position proactively. No competitor has been named 50+ days into the deal. For a $200K evaluation it is unlikely Fragua is sole-sourcing. Use the technical sessions to ask who else they are evaluating, what they use today, and why they are considering a change. Position Zycus's unified S2P suite and Merlin AI differentiation before competitors frame the narrative.
best_practice_check.summary: Three high-leverage win-strategy levers for this $200K enterprise S2P deal in active discovery: (1) Map the Economic Buyer and Decision Maker within 7 days—without Director/VP-level sponsorship, pricing and contract approval will stall; ask Miroslava directly who approves budget and request an introduction. (2) Build a quantified ROI model in the next discovery touchpoint—anchor the buyer's current-state cost drivers (PO cycle time, maverick spend, supplier onboarding delays) to Zycus's value prop and create a financial case for the $200K investment. (3) Surface the competitive landscape immediately—ask whether other vendors are being evaluated and what the current procurement/sourcing stack looks like, then position Merlin AI negotiation and iCompliance risk modules as differentiation against SAP Ariba/Coupa static workflows.

competitive_position.summary: No competitor has been identified in any available evidence (Salesforce fields, Next Steps, tasks, or Avoma calls). The deal may be sole-sourced, or the buyer has not yet disclosed their shortlist or incumbent. For a $200K enterprise S2P suite evaluation, it is statistically likely that alternatives exist but have not been surfaced. The absence of competitive intelligence 50+ days into the deal is a meaningful blind spot. Proactively positioning Zycus's differentiation (Merlin AI, unified S2P suite, LATAM experience) and discovering the competitive landscape in the post-NDA technical discussions is a strategic priority.

items.deliverable: Return signed NDA to unlock deeper process and technical discussions
items.grounding_quote: NDA review remains in progress, and we are expecting the signed agreement back as early as tomorrow. Primary goal for this session is to establish business alignment, and position Zycus for the next phase of discovery. Aligned with the client that this discussion will remain at a high level from a business perspective, while a deeper process and technical discussion will take place once the NDA is fully executed.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning strength is unknown. Product scope includes Merlin (AI negotiation agent) and Merlin Intake, signaling Zycus has positioned AI capabilities in the solution, but no recorded buyer-facing call captures the buyer's AI appetite, Merlin demo feedback, or AI-driven ROI discussion. The 15 Jun internal Zycus call mentions an AI-feature disable request for Ana Karen due to policy constraints, which is an internal Zycus matter and unrelated to this buyer's AI interest. No Salesforce AIS fields (AIS_Score__c, AIS_Status__c, AIS_Why__c) are populated. The 23 Jun discovery session objective was high-level business discovery, so AI positioning depth is likely minimal at this stage. AI positioning will be measurable after a Merlin-focused demo or use-case workshop (e.g., Merlin negotiation agent on supplier price history, Merlin Intake for autonomous requisition intake). Until then, AI fit is inferred from product scope but not validated by buyer feedback.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Customer expectations and fit criteria are in early discovery. The 23 Jun session objective (per 22 Jun Next Step) was to understand 'the initiative, key drivers, stakeholders, current challenges, and overall business objectives' at a high level, with deeper process and technical discussions gated until NDA execution. Product scope is confirmed (full S2P suite including Merlin AI), but the buyer's evaluation scorecard, priority use cases, and must-have vs nice-to-have criteria are not yet documented. No recorded call transcript captures the buyer's stated expectations. Fit assessment requires the outcome of the 23 Jun discovery session to be debriefed and documented, then validated in a follow-up use-case workshop.

hard.ais_why:
• No Avoma recordings exist for Fragua; the two scheduled meetings either had no conferencing details or were cancelled, so there is no transcript evidence of customer AI interest.
• SF next step describes a procurement transformation RFP covering sourcing, supplier risk, eProcurement, and spend analysis - broad scope but no AI, agentic, or Merlin cues in the narrative.
• Opp is brand-new (created 5/7) at Qualified with onsite discovery still to be scheduled, so absence of AI signal is a data-quality artifact more than a clear stance.

deltas.subject: Economic Buyer and Decision Maker unmapped 50 days in. For a $200K enterprise S2P deal, budget approval and contract signature require Director or VP-level sponsorship. Miroslava (Analyst) is engaged but lacks authority. Account-level contacts Julio García (Director de Cadena de Suministro) and Claudia Hermosillo (Gerente Compras Institucionales) are the likely EB/DM candidates but not yet involved. Ask Miroslava in the next touchpoint: 'Who will approve the budget and sign the contract for this initiative?' and request an introduction within 7 days. Without EB engagement in the next 14 days, pricing discussions will stall.

Zero competitive intelligence 50 days into deal. No competitor, incumbent, or alternative solution named in any field, call, or Next Step. Zycus cannot position differentiation (Merlin AI negotiation agent, iCompliance risk, ML iAnalyze spend visibility) or counter competitor narratives without knowing the landscape. Ask the buying committee in the next touchpoint (within 7 days): 'Are you evaluating other vendors alongside Zycus, and are you replacing an existing system or implementing S2P for the first time?' This surfaces the competitive field and the buyer's current-state baseline, enabling proactive positioning and wedge strategy (e.g., Merlin AI on supplier price history vs SAP Ariba's static sourcing workflows).

value.flag: Zero competitive intelligence 50 days into deal. No competitor, incumbent, or alternative solution named in any field, call, or Next Step. Zycus cannot position differentiation (Merlin AI negotiation agent, iCompliance risk, ML iAnalyze spend visibility) or counter competitor narratives without knowing the landscape. Ask the buying committee in the next touchpoint (within 7 days): 'Are you evaluating other vendors alongside Zycus, and are you replacing an existing system or implementing S2P for the first time?' This surfaces the competitive field and the buyer's current-state baseline, enabling proactive positioning and wedge strategy (e.g., Merlin AI on supplier price history vs SAP Ariba's static sourcing workflows).
